beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; yesterday's moderate energy (50/100) suggests a quieter morning routine. Clear hot day ahead means getting out early before peak heat.

beats.activity: Commute to the ER via surface streets and I-5
beats.reasoning: ER nurse commute from Wallingford to downtown; accounting for Wednesday morning traffic. Lucia knows the routes well after SR-520 closure yesterday.

beats.activity: ER nursing shift: triaging, patient care, charting
beats.reasoning: Typical 8-hour ER shift with brief breaks built in; Wednesday mid-week steady pace expected after yesterday's intensity.

beats.activity: Commute home via I-5 and Wallingford surface streets
beats.reasoning: Reverse commute; mid-afternoon traffic lighter than morning. Heading home to beat the worst heat of the day and prepare for evening plans.

beats.activity: Lunch at home: hydrate, light meal, rest from shift
beats.reasoning: Post-shift recovery; air quality and heat mean staying indoors to eat and hydrate. Lucia values quiet decompression time.

beats.activity: Gym session: weights and cardio, taking advantage of fitness obsession
beats.reasoning: Lucia is fitness-obsessed; hitting the gym before the evening concert while it's still hot but she can cool down indoors. Fits her post-shift routine.

beats.activity: Quick shower and freshen up at home
beats.reasoning: Post-gym cleanup before heading to Gas Works Park concert; short walk home from gym.

beats.activity: Drive to Gas Works Park for outdoor concert
beats.reasoning: Heading to the 6pm outdoor concert; Wallingford to Gas Works is very close (adjacent neighborhoods). Lucia values outdoor activities on clear evenings and close friends over crowds, so she'll check out early or find a quieter spot.

beats.activity: Outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; socializing and enjoying clear evening
beats.reasoning: Free concert and beautiful clear Wednesday evening; Lucia enjoys outdoor time. Even though crowds draw big attendance, she can find a quieter corner of the park with friends or solo. Air quality is moderate, manageable for outdoor activity.

beats.activity: Drive home from Gas Works Park
beats.reasoning: Short drive back to Wallingford after concert winds down; cooling evening after hot day.

beats.activity: Evening at home: write letters, play Stardew Valley, prepare home brewing setup
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening routine: Lucia writes letters by hand (recent habit from yesterday), plays video games, and finally tends to the home brewing equipment that's been calling for attention. Relaxing before sleep after a full day.
7 hours of sleep; beat spans overnight into Thursday morning. Lucia ends her day at home after a full shift, exercise, and evening leisure—ready for recovery.

diary: Long shift at Harborview today—the ER was steady, nothing catastrophic, but those five hours drain you in ways hard to explain. Triage, vitals, charting. The heat wave had people coming in dehydrated, cranky. I grabbed lunch at home and just sat with my coffee for a minute, watching the smoke haze from those BC fires drift across Wallingford. Needed to move my body, so I hit the gym harder than usual—weights, treadmill, the whole thing. Something about pushing myself after a shift helps reset the day. By 3:30 I was at Gas Works Park with a blanket and a beer, watching the light change over the water. The outdoor concert was exactly what I needed—local band, decent crowd, that perfect clear-sky evening feeling. Even the smoke couldn't ruin it. Home now, puttering around with my brewing setup and losing myself in Stardew Valley. Writing a letter to my sister. Feeling oddly settled.
thought_process: I chose the gym and the concert deliberately—knew I needed physical release after being on my feet all morning, then something gentle and social to balance it out. The heat and smoke from the fires nagged at me during my shift; I kept thinking about air quality warnings, about how these things pile up. But leaving early and getting outside turned my mood around. By the time I got to Gas Works, I wasn't thinking about work anymore. The evening felt precious in a way Wednesdays usually don't. I'm using my free time tonight to ground myself—brewing, gaming, writing—nothing demanding. Just trying to hold onto this lighter feeling before tomorrow starts again.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
